thesis_status_reasoning: The thesis is categorically falsified. All four primary pumps are offline, system pressure has collapsed to 4 PSI (90% below the regulatory minimum of 40 PSI), and Sector B hydrant pressure is at 0 PSI (100% below the fire flow minimum of 20 PSI). This simultaneously triggers three kill switches: Fire Flow Collapse, Regulatory Pressure Breach, and Divergent Inventory Paradox (97% reservoir level with catastrophic pressure loss indicates total decoupling of distribution infrastructure from supply).
action_reasoning: The system has already experienced catastrophic failure with complete loss of pumping capacity, water hammer-induced main breaks, and total loss of fire protection. Emergency shutdown protocols should formalize the failure state, prevent further infrastructure damage, and initiate emergency response coordination.

key_signals.signal: All four pumps offline with complete loss of primary pumping capacity
System pressure at 4 PSI versus 40 PSI regulatory minimum (90% shortfall)
Sector B hydrant pressure at 0 PSI versus 20 PSI fire flow minimum (complete failure)
Divergent Inventory Paradox: 97% reservoir level with 4 PSI system pressure indicates fundamental distribution failure
14 main breaks and water hammer effects indicating cascading mechanical destruction
Water turbidity at 55 NTU suggests contamination from depressurization and backflow

biggest_uncertainty: Whether the pump failures and main breaks resulted from a single catastrophic initiating event or represent cascading failures from prolonged operational stress not captured in baseline data
